title: Can I print photos added to "updates" field a board
excerpt: I need to print Work Orders which are kept in a board. I added DOCExport integration,but it still does not allow me to print photos which were adde3d to the UPDATES section.  Any suggestions?

posts.author: Bozka (Bozena Jablonski)
posts.cookedHtml: I need to print Work Orders which are kept in a board. I added DOCExport integration,but it still does not allow me to print photos which were adde3d to the UPDATES section. Any suggestions?

posts.author: Hayk (Hayk Muradyan)
posts.cookedHtml: If you want to print the photos from the update section, you will need to open it. If you click on a photo and open it at the bottom you will see print icon next to download icon.

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Bozka ,

Thanks for trying out DocExport PDF Generator.

Our app allows to print photos from the update section with the {{Body_Html}} placeholder.
Link to the help centre article: Working with item update tables.

The easiest way would be to add the photos to a file column for full flexibility: Insert images into your document

Please send your docx template and a screenshot of your board to our support team so we can help you with the structure: support@docexport.com

posts.author: Flowtech (Flowtech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Bozka

You’re right — most document export tools, including DOCExport, currently don’t support pulling images from the Updates section, which can be a bit limiting when you’re trying to generate complete Work Orders with photos attached.

If you’re looking for a solution that can pull in images from updates, file columns, or other board content and generate print-ready Work Orders (PDF or Word), check out DocCreate — a tool developed by our team specifically for this purpose.

With DocCreate, you can:

  • Pull image files from updates or file columns
  • Automatically format them into structured templates
  • Export Work Orders to PDF or Word with all content and images intact

You can install it directly from Monday with this link:
Install DocCreate on Monday

It’s built to solve exactly this kind of use case — and we’d love to hear your feedback if you try it out!
